compositions.compositionSubTitle: Symphony for big symphonic orchestra, choir, soprano, harpsichord and mandolin
compositionDescription: Written in 2011, Saga was outlining the current political state through the prism of a history.
compositions.seoDescription: “Saga Wiśniowiecka” is a 2011 symphony for orchestra, choir, soprano, harpsichord and mandolin.

compositions.compositionSubTitle: Sinfonietta for big symphonic orchestra and choir
compositionDescription: A young Iranian girl Delara Darabi took a fault for the crime made by her beloved and was executed after 5 years of prison without notification of her parents in 2009. The work highlights violence against women, describing girl’s last day at life. The first part “La victima”, the victim, describes the anticipation of a catastrophe, the second part “El hilo”, the thread of life, which may be cut off at any moment, describes the violent scene of execution. “El angelo de la muerte”, the angel of death takes her soul… The third part is a harmonization of the protestant choral, which goes in the alto and then tenor parts.
compositions.seoDescription: “Last Day” is a sinfonietta for large orchestra and choir inspired by Delara Darabi, exposing violence against women through three dramatic movements.

compositionDescription: The composition was written under the impression of one of Scandinavian tails. A beautiful girl with red hair named Cordelia is waiting for her father-mariner, who went fishing to the sea. Suddenly she sees a Queen of Fog appearing from dispersed drops of water suspended in the air. She warns the girl that her father is surrounded by thick fog and cannot see that he rushes directly to the rocks, where he’ll find his death. If the girl wants to save his life, she must sacrifice her own and give her bright beauty to the Queen of Fog. Cordelia accepts the conditions of the Queen of Fog and safely landed father finds only the disappearing ghost of his daughter when he returns home. Three parts of the composition represent three stages of the tail – a music characteristic of Cordelia in the 1st part, the appearance of the Queen of Fog, with her cold cynic sentences about possible Cordelia’s father death in the 2nd part, the dramatic transformation of Cordelia’s music characteristic in the 3rd part, being gradually “frozen” by wind instruments.
compositions.seoDescription: Discover “Rose and Fog,” a symphonic orchestra composition inspired by a Scandinavian tale, unfolding in three dramatic musical parts.

compositions.compositionSubTitle: For violin and big symphonic orchestra
compositionDescription: Human’s existence guided by a guardian angel, which appears in the celestial life after death, is the main idea of the concert.
compositions.seoDescription: Violin meets grand symphonic forces in a spiritual concerto about a guardian angel and the celestial life beyond death.

compositionDescription: The piece was written in 2 days after a crash of the airplane Tu-154 with president Lech Kaczyński on the board near Smolensk in 2010. The official version at the time was the fault of a pilot. The Polish word “Powiedzieć” means “To tell”, to tell the truth about the catastrophe, as the composer felt it was.
compositions.seoDescription: Powiedzieć (“To tell”) is a symphonic orchestra piece written after the 2010 Smolensk Tu-154 crash, an appeal to tell the truth.

compositionDescription: In a little village near the sea a mariner celebrated his marriage. He offered a beautiful scarlet scarf to his young wife. The next day, he had to go to a sea for a year. While the mariner was absent his wife gave birth to a little girl and died soon after. When the mariner returned he found the grave of his beloved wife and a little daughter, Assol.

The life of the little girl in her village wasn’t simple: the children didn’t accept her. The only friends she found were the elves in the forest near the beach. In this forest, she met a stranger, an old man who told her that one day a ship with scarlet sails will arrive and aboard the ship, there will be a young duke who will marry her. Assol believed that old man and grew up with this only hope.

The time passed and Assol reached the age of 16. She still believed in the tale told by the old stranger, so she often went to the forest near the beach to dance with her imaginative lover, but with age her notions of social stratification had been formed and the belief that some young duke could ever marry her, poor girl from a little village, also began to bring her sadness and pain. While she was crying she fell asleep in the forest. At that moment a young stranger, who just arrived with his ship and was walking nearby, appeared in the forest. His name was Gray and he was the son of a duke from the other side of the country, he had lost her mother in a very young age and had hated the high life and its notions, so he bought a ship to travel with and to find the meaning of life. In the forest, he was submerged by his thoughts about his life, about the years passed on the sea and about the sense of life he was looking for and didn’t find yet. Suddenly he remarked beautiful young girl, which were asleep on a rock. A strange feeling came to him, as he was deeply touched by her appearance and the desire to protect this little creature against all the ills of the world suddenly came to his heart. But he only took the scarlet scarf and went to the village to learn more about the girl.

In the village, he met young villagers. He began to question them about her by showing them the scarlet scarf. They mocked Assol and the story about the young duke on the ship with scarlet sails she believed. They threw the scarf on the ground and left with a laugh. Gray picked up the scarf and began to dance with – the deep and true love overflew his heart. He decided to make Assol’s dream come true and replaced the sails on his ship by scarlet sails to come to Assol.

The next day Assol awoke and went to the beach and what an astonishment she had when she saw the ship with scarlet sails approaching her! A young man with beautiful eyes full of kindness and love came to her and asked her to be his wife! It’s impossible to describe the happiness that both young people felt: Assol’s dream came true and Gray finally found the meaning of his life.
compositions.seoDescription: “Scarlet Sails” is a ballet for chamber orchestra: Assol believes in a ship with scarlet sails, until Duke Gray makes her dream come true.
